This stage sits in the end-to-end test layer for the system’s distributed runtime, validating behavior that only appears once work is split across agents, threads, jobs, and remote execution contexts. It checks that collaboration features remain coherent under realistic parent/child session flows rather than just in isolated unit boundaries.

spawn_agent_description.rs verifies the contract exposed to models when they invoke multi_agent_v1.spawn_agent, ensuring the generated tool description advertises only allowed models and gives correct guidance about overrides, reasoning effort, service tier, and authorization. agent_execution.rs then tests actual nested spawning under Collaboration V2, confirming that execution capacity is shared across descendants and that thread-limit exhaustion becomes an explicit, model-visible failure. codex_delegate.rs focuses on delegated review mode, making sure sub-agent approvals and filtered event streams are surfaced correctly in the parent thread. subagent_notifications.rs covers lifecycle and messaging details such as inherited context, hook replacement, mailbox-style notifications, and parent/child interaction traces. agent_jobs.rs validates CSV-driven job spawning, cancellation, and result persistence. remote_env.rs extends the suite into remote environments, proving execution, patching, permissions, and filesystem access stay correctly isolated from the local workspace.
